title: A cutting knife, a method for replacing a knife tip of said cutting knife, and a knife tip replacement part for use in said method
abstract: The disclosure relates to a cutting knife (100) for a cutting shaft (24) in a comminution apparatus (10), said cutting knife (100) being a one-piece element comprising a base part (110) and one or more replaceable knife tip parts (120, 120'), wherein each knife tip part is integrally formed with the base part at connection regions (130a, 130b) at an outer periphery (104) of the one-piece element and is partly separated from the base part by an associated elongate through-opening (140, 140'), said elongate through-opening: having an elongate extension (141, 141') in the main plane (102), being spaced from said outer periphery (104), and being located so as to facilitate replacement of the knife tip part (120) by reducing the cutting required to separate the knife tip part from the base part. The disclosure further relates to a method for replacing a knife tip of such a cutting knife, and a knife tip replacement part for use in said method.
claims:
Claims 1. A cutting knife (100) for a cutting shaft (24) in a comminution apparatus (10), said cutting knife (100) being a one-piece element which extends in a main plane (102) transversely to a rotational axis (R) of the cutting knife and which has an outer periphery (104) in relation to the rotational axis (R), said one-piece element comprising a base part (110) and one or more replaceable knife tip parts (120,120â), wherein said base part (110) is configured to be attached to an axle (52) of said rotatable cutting shaft (24), and wherein each of said one or more replaceable knife tip parts (120,120â) is suitable for cutting material disposed in the comminution apparatus (10), and wherein each knife tip part is integrally formed with the base part at connection regions (130a, 130b) at the outer periphery (104) and is partly separated from the base part by an associated elongate through-opening (140,140â), said elongate through-opening: having an elongate extension (141 ,141â) in the main plane (102), being spaced from said outer periphery (104), and being located so as to facilitate replacement of the knife tip part (120) by reducing the cutting required to separate the knife tip part from the base part (110). 2. The cutting knife (100) according to claim 1 , wherein the cutting knife has uniform material properties. 3. The cutting knife (100) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the cutting knife is made from metal. 4. The cutting knife (100) according to any one of claim 1 to 3, wherein the elongate extension (141 , 141â) of the elongate through-opening (140,140â) is non-linear in said main plane (102). 5. The cutting knife (100) according to any one of claim 1 to 4, wherein said elongate extension (121 ) defines: â¢ at a first connection region (130a): a first minimum distance (132a) between a first end (142a) of the elongate extension (141 ) and the outer periphery (104), and, â¢ at a second connection region (130b): a second minimum distance (132b) between a second end (142b) of the elongate extension (141 ) and the outer periphery (104), and wherein the elongate extension (141 ) is longer than the sum of said first (132a) and second (132b) minimum distances. 6. The cutting knife (100) according to any one of claim 1 to 5, wherein said elongate extension (141 ) is located such that the one or more replaceable knife tip parts (120,120â) extend so as to constitute at least 70% of the outer periphery (104) of the cutting knife. 7. The cutting knife (100) according to any one of claim 1 to 6, wherein said elongate through-opening (140) is at least partly defined by a slit (143) which forms a slit gap (144) between the base part (110) and the associated knife tip part (120) 8. The cutting knife (100) according to claim 7, wherein the slit gap (144) has a gap width (W1 ) of 0.5 - 10 mm. 9. The cutting knife (100) according to any one of claim 1 to 8, wherein said elongate through-opening (140) is further defined by one or more stress- relief openings (145a, 145b), wherein each opening of said one or more stress-relief openings (145a, 145b) is defined at an associated end (142a, 142b) of said elongate extension (141 ) so as to form a stress-relief opening gap (146a, 146b) between the base part (110) and the associated knife tip part (120), wherein the stress-relief opening gap (146a, 146b) has a gap width (W2a,W2b) being larger than a gap width (W1 ) of a center portion (147) of the elongate through-opening (140), said one or more stress-relief 

 openings (145a, 145b) being structured and arranged to spatially distribute internal forces induced in the cutting knife when in use. 10. The cutting knife (100) according to claim 9, wherein the one or more stress-relief openings (145a, 145b) have rounded, preferably circular, cross sections (148a, 148b) as defined in said main plane (102). 1 1. The cutting knife (100) according to any one of claim 1 to 10, wherein said one or more replaceable knife tip parts comprises two replaceable knife tip parts (120, 120â) being disposed on opposite sides of the base part (1 10). 12. The cutting knife (100) according to any one of claim 1 to 1 1 , wherein said connection regions (130a, 130b) being provided with one or more markings (134a, 134b), such as visual indications, providing one or more guides for intended cutting lines for said cutting required to separate said one or more knife tip parts (120, 120â) from the base part (1 10). 13. The cutting knife (200) according to any one of claim 1 to 12, wherein the elongate through-opening (240) has an inner surface (249) which includes surface portions (249a, 249b) extending along the elongate extension (241 ), wherein said surface portions (249a, 249b) are three-dimensionally shaped, as defined in the main plane (102) and along a direction of the rotational axis (R). 14. The cutting knife (200) according to claim 13, wherein the elongate through-opening (240), at each position along the elongate extension (241 ) of the opening, extends through the cutting knife along a specific direction (A) which forms an angle (B) with the rotational axis (R), wherein said angle (B) varies as function of position along the elongate extension (241 ). 15. A rotatable cutting shaft (24) for a comminution apparatus (10), comprising: 

 an axle (25) extending along a rotational axis (S) of the rotatable cutting shaft (24), and a plurality of cutting knives (100) according to any one of claim 1 to 14, said cutting knifes being attached to the axle (25) and being mutually spaced along the rotational axis of the cutting shaft (24). 16. A comminution apparatus (10) comprising one or more rotatable cutting shafts (24) according to claim 15. 17. A method for replacing a knife tip part (120) of a cutting knife (100) according to any one of claim 1 to 14, said method comprising: separating a knife tip part (120) of the cutting knife from the base part (1 10) of the cutting knife by cutting said one-piece element at said connection regions (130a, 130b) where said knife tip part is integrally formed with the base part, said cutting forming two through-cuts (150a, 150b), removing the separated knife tip part (120) so as to reveal, on the base part (120), a base part mounting interface (152), said base part mounting interface presenting two cut surfaces (154a, 154b) corresponding to said two through-cuts (152a, 152b), and one pre-engineered surface (156) forming part of the elongate through-opening (140); positioning a knife tip replacement part (1000), which has a replacement part mounting interface (1052), in the same place as the previously removed knife tip part (120) such that the replacement part mounting interface (1052) is aligned with the base part mounting interface (152); and attaching the knife tip replacement part (1000) to the base part (1 10) by welding together the knife tip replacement part with the base part along at least parts of said base part mounting interface (152) and said replacement part mounting interface (1052). 18. The method according to claim 17, wherein said attaching the knife tip replacement part (1000) to the base part (1 10) is performed by welding together the knife tip replacement part (1000) and the base part (1 10) only at, or along boundaries of, said two cut surfaces (154a, 154b) of the base part, so 

 as to form one or more new elongate through-openings (160a, 160b) between the welded-together knife tip replacement part (1000) and base part (1 10). 19. The method according to claim 17 or 18, wherein the replacement part mounting interface (1052) is structured and arranged to fit to the base part mounting interface (152) such that the replacement part mounting interface (1052) and the base part mounting interface (152) together define a mutual transition interface between the base part and the knife tip replacement part. 20. The method according to any one of claim 17 to 19, wherein the knife tip replacement part (1000), at the replacement part mounting interface (1052), presents two or more positioning protrusions (1060a, 1060b, 1060c) adapted to be brought into abutment with surfaces of the base part (1 10) along the base part mounting interface (152) when positioning the knife tip replacement part (1000). 21. The method according to claim 20, wherein said positioning protrusions (1060a, 1060b, 1060c) are arranged such that they will be in abutment with the pre-engineered surface (156) of the base part (1 10). 22. A knife tip replacement part (1000) for the replacement of a knife tip part (120) of a cutting knife (100) according to any one of claim 1 to 14, said knife tip replacement part extending in a main plane (1020) and presenting: one or more cutting teeth (1010a, 1010b); and a replacement part mounting interface (1052) structured and arranged to be aligned with a base part mounting interface (152) of a base part (1 10), wherein said replacement part mounting interface (1052) comprises: â¢ two or more positioning protrusions (1060a, 1060b, 1060c) which are located at mutually spaced positions in the main plane (1020) and adapted to be in abutment with the pre-engineered surface (156) of the disconnected base part (1 10) when the 

 knife tip replacement part (1000) is correctly positioned in relation to the disconnected base part (110); and â¢ two or more attachment portions (1070a, 1070b) being adapted to be attached by welding to associated cut surfaces (154a, 154b) of the base part (110). 23. The knife tip replacement part according to claim 22, wherein the knife tip replacement part (1000) presents, at each attachment portion (1070a, 1070b), a respective stress-relief opening (1080a, 1080b) structured and arranged to spatially distribute internal stress forces induced in the knife tip replacement part (1000), the base part (110), and a weld (190a, 190b) attaching said knife tip replacement part and base part together, when in use. 24. The knife tip replacement part according to claim 23, wherein each stress-relief opening (1080a, 1080b) is defined by an inwardly projecting opening which protrudes into the knife tip replacement part (1000) next to the respective attachment portion (1070a, 1070b) and which opens into the replacement part mounting interface (1052). 25. A knife tip replacement part (1000) for the replacement of a knife tip part (120) of a cutting knife (100), said knife tip replacement part (1000) extending in a main plane (1020) and comprising: one or more cutting teeth (1010a, 1010b); and a replacement part mounting interface (1052) structured and arranged for mounting the knife tip replacement part (1000); wherein said mounting interface (1052) comprises: two or more positioning protrusions (1060a, 1060b, 1060c) which are located at mutually spaced positions in the main plane (1020) and protrude parallel to the main plane, and two or more attachment portions (1070a, 1070b) being adapted to be attached by welding to associated cut surfaces (154a, 154b) of the base part (110). 26. A method for manufacturing a cutting knife (100) according to any one of claim 1 to 14 from a precursor one-piece element (100P) which extends in the main plane (102) and which has an outer periphery (104P) in relation to a rotational axis (P) of said precursor one-piece element (100P), the method comprising: cutting through the precursor one-piece element (100P) so as to create, for each replaceable knife tip part (120,120â) of the cutting knife, an elongate through-opening (140,140â), wherein said elongate through-opening has an elongate extension (141 ,141â) in the main plane (102), is spaced from said outer periphery (104P), and is located so as to facilitate replacement of the knife tip part (120,120â) by reducing the cutting required to separate the knife tip part (120, 120â) from the base part (110). 27. The method according to claim 26, wherein the precursor one- piece element (100P) is a steel element, and the cutting of said elongate through-opening (140,140â) is achieved using a flame cutter (80).
